title: Corrections
description: Spec-silent shape (spec sec-5 names 'corrections.json' without fields; the plan's Phase 2 section fixes the minimal shape used here, logged in IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG.md). Top-level array published at content/corrections.json, the public corrections log linked from every card footer. Phase 1 ships this schema only -- entries are appended by the Phase 2 analyst when a pending correction (data/pending_corrections.json) is confirmed against fresh evidence. Phase 6 adds optional target_type/target_id so a correction can name a content/companies/<id>.json profile as well as a card, without breaking any existing consumer: card_id stays required and is exactly what today's pipeline (the analyst prompt, scripts/pending_corrections.py, site/builders/corrections.py) reads and writes for a card-targeted correction; target_type/target_id are an additive, currently-optional pair (present together or not at all, enforced via dependentRequired) that a future stage can wire up for company-targeted corrections. No live data exists in content/corrections.json as of Phase 6 (empty array), so this is a forward-looking schema addition, not a migration of real rows.

card_id.description: content/cards/<id>.json this correction applies to. Remains required for backward compatibility with the pipeline in place today, which only ever targets cards -- see target_type/target_id below for the forward-looking company-targeting hook.

target_type.description: Optional (Phase 6, forward-looking): what kind of record target_id names. Must be present together with target_id or not at all. Not yet populated by any current writer -- card_id above remains the field actually in use for today's card-only corrections workflow.

target_id.description: Optional (Phase 6, forward-looking): the id of the record this correction targets -- a content/cards/<id>.json id when target_type is 'card', or a content/companies/<id>.json id when target_type is 'company'. Must be present together with target_type or not at all.
